title: Server Manager App Helper Contract
description: Uniform helper/health endpoint every managed application exposes so Server Manager can interface with it. Full instructions: /integrate/llms.txt

auth.description: Per-request HMAC signature so the shared secret never travels on the wire and captured requests cannot be replayed.

headers.X-Srvmgr-Timestamp: unix seconds; reject if more than 300s from now
headers.X-Srvmgr-Nonce: random hex, single-use (optionally cache to block same-window replay)

legacy_fallback.note: Still accepted for older helpers; prefer the signature.

logs.description: Recent request/access log lines for the Server Manager traffic map. Powers per-app attribution, ISP/country/URL/volume breakdowns and flow arcs.

enroll.description: One-time pairing handshake. Called by Server Manager WITHOUT the shared secret (it does not hold it yet). The helper verifies the challenge it displayed AND, when it was unlocked with a signed manager token, a matching manager-signed claim, then returns the shared secret exactly once and permanently disables pairing.

challenge.description: single-use key from the helper's pairing page (also embedded in the enrollment payload)

jti.description: the manager unlock token id echoed by the app's enrollment payload

claim.description: base64url(json).ed25519_sig — a manager-signed claim token {v:2,typ:'claim',jti,challenge,exp}; REQUIRED when the app was unlocked with a signed token

secret.description: the app's self-generated shared secret; stored by the manager as helper_token

pairing.summary: Gated, zero-touch, cryptographically air-tight enrollment. No shared secret is ever copied by a human, an unpaired helper never leaks anything to a stranger, and every step is bound by Ed25519 signatures + a single-use token id (jti).
pairing.trust_anchor: The manager owns an Ed25519 keypair. Its PUBLIC key is baked into helpers downloaded from /integrate/helper.php (and served at GET /api/pair/pubkey), so a helper can verify manager tokens OFFLINE — no interceptable callback.
flow: Helper auto-generates a 256-bit secret AND its own Ed25519 keypair on first run, stored outside the web root.
The helper's pairing page is LOCKED. In Server Manager → Apps → Pair app the operator generates a short-lived, manager-SIGNED unlock token (carries a unique jti + expiry).

The operator pastes that token on the app's helper page. The helper verifies the manager's signature against the pinned public key (offline) — proving it came from this manager and is unexpired/unused — then records the jti.

Once unlocked, the helper shows ONE self-signed enrollment payload (see enrollment_payload) that bundles the URL, challenge, host, the app's public key and the jti. The operator copies just this and pastes it into Server Manager.

The manager verifies the payload signature, checks the jti matches a live token it issued, then POSTs {action:'enroll', challenge, jti, claim} to the helper over HTTPS. The claim is a manager-signed token bound to the same jti + challenge.
The helper verifies the manager-signed claim before handing over the secret once, then disables further pairing. The manager stores the secret as helper_token and consumes the jti (single use).

enrollment_payload.description: The SINGLE required value the operator copies from the app's unlocked helper page into the manager. Self-signed by the app so tampering is detected.
enrollment_payload.signed_by: the app's own Ed25519 key (public half is included as app_pub for trust-on-first-use)

json.jti: id of the manager unlock token that opened the gate (binds app↔token)
json.app_pub: the app's Ed25519 public key, base64url

pubkey_endpoint.auth: none (public); public keys are safe to expose

verify_endpoint.note: Legacy/online fallback. Modern helpers verify the signed token offline and do not call this.

request.code: the unlock token (or legacy PIN) the operator was shown in the manager

env.SRVMGR_MANAGER_URL: manager base URL; used to fetch/pin the public key and for the online verify fallback
env.SRVMGR_MANAGER_PUBKEY: manager Ed25519 public key (base64url); baked in on download, enables OFFLINE token verification
env.SRVMGR_PAIR_CODE: optional static unlock PIN for the legacy online flow when no signing key is available
env.SRVMGR_HELPER_TOKEN: optional: set the secret explicitly instead of auto-generating
env.SRVMGR_STATE_DIR: optional: persistent, non-web-served dir for the secret, app keypair + pairing state

pairing.re_pair: Delete pairing.json in the state dir to re-arm pairing (e.g. when moving managers).

registration_fields.name: string
registration_fields.domain: public domain for HTTPS helper calls
registration_fields.helper_token: shared secret; must match the endpoint

security: Prefer the HMAC signature; verify it in constant time (hash_equals).
Reject requests whose X-Srvmgr-Timestamp is more than 300s from now.
Optionally cache X-Srvmgr-Nonce values for 5 min and reject duplicates (replay defense).
The shared secret is the HMAC key and must never be sent in a header or logged.
Legacy X-Srvmgr-Token fallback is constant-time compared; remove it once all callers sign.
For the logs action, return only metadata (ip/path/status/bytes) — never secrets or request bodies.
Endpoint uses the app's own DB credentials; never transmit them.
Catch exceptions; return status:degraded instead of stack traces.
